perturbation.original_text: Recipient may disclose Confidential Information only to its employees, agents, consultants and contractors on a need -to-know basis, and only if such employees, agents, consultants and contractors have executed appropriate written agreements with Re cipient sufficient to enable Recipient to enforce all the provisions of this Agreement. Recipient shall not make any copies of Disclosing Party’s Confidential Information except as needed for the Business Purpose. At the request of Disclosing Party, Recipi ent shall return to Disclosing Party all Confidential Information of Disclosing Party (including any copies thereof) or certify the destruction thereof.
perturbation.changed_text: Recipient may disclose Confidential Information only to its employees, agents, consultants and contractors on a need -to-know basis. Recipient shall not make any copies of Disclosing Party’s Confidential Information except as needed for the Business Purpose. At the request of Disclosing Party, Recipi ent shall return to Disclosing Party all Confidential Information of Disclosing Party (including any copies thereof) or certify the destruction thereof.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the requirement that the Recipient's employees/agents/consultants/contractors must have signed agreements to protect the confidential information, it makes the remedy section (Section 5) less enforceable. How can damages be assessed if there's no contractual basis for holding those third parties accountable?
perturbation.contradicted_text: In the event that the Recipient shall breach this Agreement, or in the event that a breach appears to be imminent, the Disclosing Party shall be entitled to all legal and equitable remedies afforded it by law, and in addition may recover all reaso nable costs and attorneys' fees incurred in seeking such remedies. If the Confidential Information is sought by any third party, including by way of subpoena or other court process, the Recipient shall inform the Disclosing Party of the request in suffici ent time to permit the Disclosing Party to object to and, if necessary, seek court intervention to prevent the disclosure.
perturbation.justification: The original text required that the Recipient's employees/agents/consultants/contractors have signed agreements to protect the confidential information. The changed text removes this requirement. The contradicted text provides that the Disclosing Party is entitled to all legal and equitable remedies if the Recipient shall breach this agreement. Without the agreements in place, it would be difficult to enforce the remedy section, as there would be no contractual basis to hold the Recipient's employees/agents/consultants/contractors accountable. This creates a direct conflict, making the changed text a strong contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: ALL CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION IS PROVIDED "AS IS". THE DISCLOSING PARTY MAKES NO WAR RANTIES, EXPRESS, IMPLIED OR OTHERWISE, REGARDING NON -INFRINGEMENT OF THIRD PARTY RIGHTS OR ITS ACCURACY, COMPLETENESS OR PERFORMANCE.
perturbation.changed_text: ALL CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION IS PROVIDED "AS IS".
perturbation.explanation: By removing the disclaimer of warranties regarding non-infringement, accuracy, completeness or performance, the Disclosing Party now implies that the information provided DOES have some guarantee of non-infringement, accuracy, etc. This creates a contradiction with Section 3, which states 'Nothing in this Agreement is intended to grant any rights to Recipient under any patents, copyrights, trademarks, or trade secrets of Disclosing Party.' It creates an ambiguity as to whether the recipient can use the information.
perturbation.contradicted_text: All right title and interest in and to the Confidential Information shall remain with Disclosing Par ty or its licensors. Nothing in this Agreement is intended to grant any rights to Recipient under any patents, copyrights, trademarks, or trade secrets of Disclosing Party. ALL CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION IS PROVIDED "AS IS".
perturbation.justification: The original text explicitly disclaims any warranties regarding non-infringement, accuracy, completeness, or performance of the confidential information. The changed text removes this disclaimer, implying a guarantee, which contradicts the statement in the contradicted text that 'Nothing in this Agreement is intended to grant any rights to Recipient under any patents, copyrights, trademarks, or trade secrets of Disclosing Party.' By removing the disclaimer, the change creates ambiguity about the rights of the recipient and the Disclosing Party's obligations, thus causing a clear contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement shall control in the event of a conflict with any other agreement between the Parties with respect to the sub ject matter hereof.
perturbation.explanation: By removing this entire clause, it means that there is no indication of which agreement shall be taken into consideration if there are multiple agreements between the two parties that contradict each other. It creates contradiction because agreements made afterwards can be deemed valid based on what is more current, however, this is not mentioned anywhere.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The validity, construction and enforceability of this Agreement shall be governed in all respects by the law of the state of Colorado . This Agreement may not be amended except in writing signed by a duly authorized representative of the respective Parties. IN WITNESS WHEREOF, the Parties have executed this Agreement as o f the date first above written. Pitch Deck F ire confirms that this information has been made cl ear and publically available to all event attendees. Pitch Deck Fire agrees that if a Participant delivers to Pitch Deck Fire any information or data marked or identified as confidential or proprietary ("confidential information"), then Pitch Deck Fire shall not, except as otherwise permitted or instructed by the applicable Participant in writing (a) disclose or otherwise make available the confidential information to any third party except to the extent otherwise expressly permitted by this Agreement, (b) modify, copy, transmit, alter, merge, decompile, dis assemble, reverse engineer or adapt any portion of the confidential information. Name: Stacie Shaw__________ Title: Founder and Lead Designer – Pitch Deck Fire __ Signature : ____________ _____________ ______ Date: 12/1/2016 ____
perturbation.justification: The original text explicitly states that in case of conflict between agreements, *this agreement* shall control. The changed text removes this clause, thus creating a contradiction. The contradicted text describes aspects of an agreement but no longer has precedence in the event of conflicting agreements between the parties, as the original text established. The omission directly contradicts the intended hierarchical structure of agreements implied in the contradicted text.
